title: Lesson Quiz: Fiscal Policy
description: Assessment of the concept that Government spending and taxation decisions.

choices.text: The use of government spending and taxation decisions to influence the economy
choices.feedback: Correct. Fiscal policy uses spending and tax decisions to affect economic activity.

choices.text: The study of how businesses advertise products
The collection of property taxes only
choices.feedback: Fiscal policy includes broader spending and tax decisions, not just one tax type.
That describes regulation, not fiscal policy.

questions.prompt: Expansionary fiscal policy may include higher government spending or lower taxes.

choices.feedback: Correct. Expansionary fiscal policy is used to encourage economic activity.
This is incorrect. Expansionary policy often uses more spending or lower taxes.

questions.prompt: Which action best represents contractionary fiscal policy?

choices.text: Increasing spending on public construction during a slowdown
choices.feedback: That is more closely related to expansionary fiscal policy.

choices.text: Lowering taxes so households keep more of their income
choices.feedback: That is also more closely related to expansionary policy.

choices.text: Reducing government spending or raising taxes to slow activity
choices.feedback: Correct. Contractionary fiscal policy is meant to slow overall economic activity.

choices.text: Requiring businesses to test products for safety
choices.feedback: That is a regulation example, not fiscal policy.

questions.prompt: How can fiscal policy influence the economy?

choices.text: By changing government spending and tax decisions that affect economic activity
choices.feedback: Correct. Fiscal policy can influence spending, production, and other economic behavior.

choices.text: By deciding what color businesses must paint their buildings
choices.feedback: That does not describe fiscal policy.

choices.text: By replacing all consumer decisions with government decisions
choices.feedback: Fiscal policy influences the economy, but it does not replace all private decisions.

choices.text: By focusing only on property tax values each year
choices.feedback: Fiscal policy is broader than one tax category.

questions.prompt: Fiscal policy is different from simply identifying types of taxes because it focuses on how government uses spending and taxation decisions as a strategy.

choices.feedback: Correct. Fiscal policy is about strategy and economic influence, not just naming tax types.
This is incorrect. Fiscal policy focuses on how government uses spending and taxes to influence the economy.
